prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `4b6b68e5`.
codex_response:
Implemented `4b6b68e5` end-to-end. The corrected rule is: keep every multi-cell single-color outline component, fill only the genuinely enclosed regions of closed outlines, and use the dominant non-background, non-outline color found strictly inside that enclosed region; isolated singleton noise is removed. I discarded the summaries as authoritative: `opus` had the right fill idea but wrongly implied open components are discarded, and `sonnet` overfit the task to rectangles, so I documented the mismatch in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_4b6b68e5/notes.md).

The generator matches the family by mixing closed rectangle frames, irregular closed orthogonal outlines, and open rectangle-like outlines, then adding sparse isolated singleton markers and distractors. I did not add any primitive to `arc2/dsl_ext.py`; the only shared task-local helper is enclosed/boundary detection. Official verification passed `4/4`, the shared build produced 1000 examples, generated verification passed `1000/1000`, and I visually checked the preview sheet.
